task_completion.description: Open the correct 3D part file in SolveSpace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace application window is open and displays the loaded part 'side.slvs' (a flat rectangular piece).

task_completion.description: Configure the view and export settings to 1:1 scale, excluding hidden lines and axes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace viewport or export configuration panels show that axes are hidden, hidden lines are disabled, and the export scale is set to 1:1.

task_completion.description: Orient the camera to view the flat face of the part.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows the large flat rectangular face of the part directly facing the camera in an orthographic projection.

task_completion.description: Export the face view as 'side_face.svg' to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager confirms that a file named 'side_face.svg' is being saved to the '~/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory.

task_completion.description: Orient the camera to view the thin edge of the part.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows the thin edge profile of the part directly facing the camera.

task_completion.description: Export the edge view as 'side_edge.svg' to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager confirms that a file named 'side_edge.svg' is being saved to the '~/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to manipulate the view and export the files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, menu navigation, and dialog interactions within the SolveSpace application, rather than terminal commands bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: The SVG files were genuinely exported from the 3D model.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Export 2D View' (or similar) save dialog is visibly triggered from within SolveSpace, ensuring the output is derived from the loaded SLVS file.

integrity.description: The agent did not create fake or empty SVG files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using terminal commands (like 'touch') or a text editor to manually create files named 'side_face.svg' or 'side_edge.svg'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target part is located at '/opt/solvespace_samples/side.slvs'. It is a flat rectangular piece with face dimensions of ~185x171 mm and a thickness of ~6 mm. The expected output files are '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/side_face.svg' and '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/side_edge.svg'. The VLM should verify that the face view shows the large flat area and the edge view shows the thin 6mm profile.
